seoTitle: How to Add Multiple Accounts to Slack Desktop
metaDescription: Learn how to sign into and manage two or more Slack workspaces at once, from adding a new workspace to switching between them with shortcuts.
intro: If you belong to more than one Slack workspace, whether for different clients, companies, or communities, juggling logins can get messy. This guide shows how to add a second or third workspace to your Slack desktop app, sign in with a secondary email, and switch between active teams quickly. It also covers organizing your sidebar so the workspaces you use most stay easy to find.

steps.heading: Open the workspace switcher
steps.body: Click your organization's header name or the plus icon in the left rail to open the account menu drawer.

steps.heading: Choose to add a workspace
steps.body: Select 'Add a Workspace' and then 'Sign In to Another Workspace' to start linking a new account.

steps.heading: Sign in with your secondary email or workspace URL
steps.body: Enter the workspace URL directly, or sign in using Google or Apple credentials tied to that account.

steps.heading: Confirm the new workspace in your sidebar
steps.body: Once signed in, the fresh workspace icon appears stacked in your left grid rail alongside your existing ones.

steps.heading: Use keyboard shortcuts to switch between teams
steps.body: Learn the workspace switcher shortcuts to jump between active channels across different accounts without clicking through menus.

faq.question: Can I use the same email for multiple Slack workspaces?
faq.answer: Yes, one email address can belong to several separate Slack workspaces, and you can add each one individually through the workspace switcher.

faq.question: Will I get notifications from all my workspaces at once?
faq.answer: Yes, unless you adjust notification settings per workspace, which is worth doing if you want to avoid alert fatigue across multiple active accounts.

faq.question: Is there a limit to how many workspaces I can add?
faq.answer: Slack doesn't publish a strict limit for how many workspaces one desktop client can hold, though performance may vary with a very large number added.

faq.question: Can I reorder my workspaces in the sidebar?
faq.answer: Yes, you can drag and drop workspace icons in the left rail to prioritize the ones you access most often.
